name: Pygmy Goat
info: Small, friendly goats perfect for petting zoos. They love to climb and play.

name: Alpaca
info: Gentle, fluffy animals from South America. Their soft wool is highly valued.

name: Babydoll Southdowns Sheep
info: Adorable miniature sheep with woolly faces. Very docile and great with children.

name: Rabbit
info: Fast-breeding, adorable mammals. Children love watching them hop around.

name: Meerkat
info: Highly social desert mammals known for standing upright and acting as sentinels.

name: Common Peafowl
info: Stunning birds famous for the male's iridescent tail feathers displayed in elaborate courtship.

name: Chicken
info: The world's most common domesticated bird. Great for educational farm exhibits.

name: Emu
info: Australia's largest bird and second-largest in the world. Fast runners with curious personalities.

name: Fallow Deer
info: Elegant deer with distinctive palmate antlers. Common in European parks and estates.

name: Tortoise
info: Long-lived reptiles with protective shells. Some species can live over 100 years.

name: Capybara
info: The world's largest rodent. Highly social and semi-aquatic, known for their calm demeanor.

name: Red Panda
info: Adorable arboreal mammals native to the Himalayas. Despite their name, they're not related to giant pandas.

name: Giant Panda
info: China's beloved national treasure. These gentle giants spend most of their time eating bamboo.

name: African Buffalo
info: Massive, powerful bovines known as 'black death' due to their unpredictable nature.

name: African Elephant
info: The largest land animal on Earth. Highly intelligent with complex social structures.

name: African Lion
info: The king of the jungle. The only truly social big cat, living in prides.

name: African Warthog
info: Ugly but endearing pigs with distinctive facial warts. Made famous by Pumbaa in The Lion King.

name: American Bighorn Sheep
info: Sure-footed mountain dwellers famous for their massive curved horns and head-butting contests.

name: American Bison
info: North America's largest land mammal. Once nearly extinct, now a symbol of the American West.

name: Arctic Wolf
info: White wolves adapted to extreme cold. They hunt in packs and are highly social.

name: Bengal Tiger
info: The most numerous tiger subspecies. Solitary hunters known for their orange coats and black stripes.

name: Black Bear
info: North America's most common bear. Despite their name, they can be brown, cinnamon, or blonde.

name: Black Leopard
info: Melanistic leopards with black coats. Their spots are still visible in bright light.

name: Black Rhinoceros
info: Hook-lipped rhinos known for their aggressive temperament. Critically endangered due to poaching.

name: California Sea Lion
info: Highly intelligent marine mammals known for their playful behavior and loud barking calls.

name: Cheetah
info: The fastest land animal, capable of speeds over 70 mph. Built for sprinting, not endurance.

name: Chimpanzee
info: Our closest living relatives. Highly intelligent with complex social behaviors and tool use.

name: Clouded Leopard
info: Medium-sized cats with beautiful cloud-like markings. Excellent climbers with the longest canine teeth relative to skull size.

name: Common Wildebeest
info: Antelopes famous for their massive annual migration across the Serengeti.

name: Dromedary Camel
info: One-humped camels adapted to arid environments. Can go weeks without water.

name: Emperor Penguin
info: The tallest and heaviest penguin species. Famous for their epic breeding marches across Antarctic ice.

name: Gemsbok
info: Also known as the South African Oryx. Striking antelopes with long, straight horns.

name: Giant Anteater
info: Unique mammals with long snouts and tongues adapted for eating ants and termites.

name: Giraffe
info: The tallest living terrestrial animal. Their long necks allow them to reach leaves other herbivores can't.

name: Gray Wolf
info: Highly social pack hunters. The largest wild member of the dog family.

name: Greater Flamingo
info: Tall, pink wading birds known for their distinctive curved bills and flamingo pink coloration from their diet.

name: Grizzly Bear
info: North American subspecies of brown bear. Powerful and aggressive when threatened.

name: Hippopotamus
info: Massive semi-aquatic mammals. Despite their chubby appearance, they're one of Africa's most dangerous animals.

name: Ibex
info: Wild goats with impressive curved horns. Masters of climbing steep, rocky terrain.

name: Jaguar
info: The largest cat in the Americas. Powerful swimmers with the strongest bite of all big cats.

name: Leopard
info: Adaptable big cats found throughout Africa and Asia. Excellent climbers who store prey in trees.

name: Lowland Gorilla
info: The largest living primates. Gentle giants with complex social structures led by a silverback male.

name: Mandrill
info: Colorful primates with striking red and blue faces. The largest of all monkeys.

name: Markhor
info: Wild goats with distinctive corkscrew horns. Pakistan's national animal.

name: Moose
info: The largest member of the deer family. Solitary animals with massive palmate antlers.

name: Okapi
info: Giraffe's only living relative. Zebra-striped legs and shy nature make them a zoo favorite.

name: Olive Baboon
info: Highly social primates living in large troops. One of the most widespread monkey species in Africa.

name: Ostrich
info: The world's largest bird. Flightless but incredibly fast runners.

name: Plains Zebra
info: Iconic African horses with distinctive black and white stripes. Each zebra's stripe pattern is unique.

name: Polar Bear
info: The largest land carnivore. Perfectly adapted to life in the Arctic with thick fur and blubber.

name: Red Kangaroo
info: The largest of all kangaroos. Males are reddish-brown while females are blue-gray.

name: Saltwater Crocodile
info: The largest of all living reptiles. Apex predators with the strongest bite force ever recorded.

name: Siberian Tiger
info: The largest tiger subspecies. Adapted to cold climates with thick fur and a stocky build.

name: Snow Leopard
info: Elusive mountain cats with thick, smoky-gray coats. Known as 'ghosts of the mountains'.

name: Spotted Hyena
info: Highly social predators living in female-dominated clans. Powerful jaws can crush bone.

name: Thomson's Gazelle
info: Small, fast antelopes famous for their bounding 'stotting' gait to evade predators.

name: Triceratops
info: Three-horned dinosaurs from the late Cretaceous period. One of the most recognizable dinosaurs.

name: White Bengal Tiger
info: Rare white variant of the Bengal tiger. The result of a rare genetic mutation, not a separate subspecies.

name: Unicorn
info: Legendary horse-like creatures with a single spiraling horn. Symbols of purity and grace.
